new_classes.label_en: Purified Undenatured Ethyl Alcohol of Less Than 80% vol in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22089099)
new_classes.label_de: Gereinigter unvergällter Ethylalkohol mit weniger als 80 % vol in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22089099)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80 % vol, obtained not exclusively through fermentation but also through a process of purification causing the product to lose the properties and characteristics of fermented beverages, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, classified under CN code 2208 90 99.

A process applied after fermentation that removes the organoleptic characteristics and properties of the fermented beverage from the product, resulting in a purified ethyl alcohol of less than 80% vol that is no longer classifiable as a fermented beverage.

new_properties.label_en: lost fermented beverage characteristics through purification

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) undenatured ethyl alcohol below 80% vol; (2) obtained not exclusively by fermentation but also through a purification process; (3) the purification causes loss of fermented beverage properties/characteristics; (4) container size > 2 litres (distinguishing 22089099 from its sibling 22089091, which covers ≤ 2 L containers). The 'retainsOrganolepticalCharacteristics = false' hasValue restriction models the loss of fermented beverage characteristics. Minor gap: the legal text is truncated and may contain additional qualifying criteria not visible in the provided snippet (e.g. specific alcohol strength range thresholds or product categories) — hence score of 0.85 rather than 1.0. The sibling code 22089091 (containers ≤ 2 L) is not modelled here but shares most axioms.
